title: Pfeiffer EJN2018 bilateral lateral trajectory profile
citation: Pfeiffer, C., Noel, J. P., Serino, A., & Blanke, O. (2018). Vestibular modulation of peripersonal space boundaries. European Journal of Neuroscience, 47(7), 800-811.
notes: Trajectory/noise profile from the local Pfeiffer_EJN2018 simulator reference. It encodes pink-noise lateral movement geometry, mirrored left-to-right/right-to-left source variants, and sample rate; FABIAN HRIR rendering remains the standardized under-the-hood app resource, not a Pfeiffer-specific user choice.

reference_parameters.level_note: The reference MATLAB script applies dB gain terms before final peak normalization; these values describe relative level shaping, not a calibrated SPL in the WAV file.

provenance.source: The MATLAB reference uses only x/y coordinates: ears at [-head_diam/2, 0] and [+head_diam/2, 0], source path [x, ordinate]. There is no vertical coordinate, so the matching 3D convention is z = 0 at the ear/head-center plane.
Reference variable `ordinate = 0.05`, described as the distance between the source and the head at 0 degrees.
Reference variables `start = -0.4`, `stop = -start`, and `speed = 0.2`; movement duration is abs(stop-start)/speed = 4 s.
Reference script uses pink noise synthesis via a 1/F filter at 44100 Hz.
Reference variable `head_diam = 0.18`; the 3DTI listener is created with matching head radius 0.09 m for customized ITD.

Reference variables `ref_dB = 100`, `att_factor = 40`, directional dB loss equations for each ear, and final `output_F = 0.25 * output_F ./ max(max(output_F))`; because of final peak normalization, the WAV is not absolutely SPL calibrated.
